arts.ttl: Live Blog: Crystal Palace v Millwall
arts.sum: Crystal Palace are taking on Millwall at Selhurst Park for a place in the FA Cup quarter-finals – follow the match LIVE below!

arts.ttl: Oliver Glasner confirms Matt Turner will start Crystal Palace's FA Cup clash with Millwall
arts.sum: Nottingham Forest loanee accepts role behind Dean Henderson in the Premier League

arts.ttl: Oliver Glasner has outlined his big Crystal Palace wish ahead of Millwall showdown
arts.sum: Crystal Palace boss will face his first South London derby in the FA Cup sixth round tie at Selhurst Park on Saturday and OLiver Glasner is looking to take Palace to Wembley

arts.ttl: Glasner reveals goalkeeper choice ahead of Millwall clash
arts.sum: Speaking in his pre-Millwall press conference, Oliver Glasner gave an insight into his team selection, and his thoughts on the new technology which will be in use in FA Cup fifth-round tie.

arts.ttl: Oliver Glasner to go full strength against Millwall - but there will be Crystal Palace goalkeeper change
arts.sum: He deserves some games and that’s why he will start tomorrow.” Oliver Glasner says his Crystal Palace side will not need a rest against Millwall but will change the starting goalkeeper for Saturday’s FA Cup clash.

arts.ttl: Japhet Tanganga relishing Crystal Palace test after finding solace at Millwall
arts.sum: Japhet Tanganga is enjoying a new lease of life at Millwall after leaving Tottenham Hotspur in the summer and will be a key man in their big FA Cup clash with Crystal Palace
Eight months after the goalkeeper's sudden death at the age of 26, Matija Sarkic's memory lives on at Millwall.

arts.ttl: 'The sky's the limit for him': Marc Guehi says Chelsea have a top talent in their ranks with an incredibly bright future
arts.sum: Chelsea are in a season of transition at present and there’s certainly plenty of cause for optimism going forward. The Blues’ form has wobbled somewhat...

arts.ttl: Jeffrey Schlupp set for Celtic transfer sit down with Brendan Rodgers as Greg Taylor offered advice
arts.sum: Schlupp's loan deal is up in the summer while Taylor is back to work after becoming a dad

arts.ttl: Millwall bring biggest-small-club-in-the-world energy to Selhurst Park | Barney Ronay
arts.sum: A club now safely moored after years of instability renews their slow-burn rivalry with Crystal Palace in FA Cup “By undertaking a Freudian analysis of Millwall fandom, combined with a Lacanian interpretation of the death drive … it is possible to perceive Millwall fandom as a form of symbolic masochism.” Hmm. OK then. That sounds, on balance, like a strong disapprove. Although to be fair the walk from New Cross can be a bit bleak at this time of year. The great days of anthropologically vital city-centre rumbles may be long gone, but there is unlikely to be a shortage of this kind of stuff any time soon. Continue reading...
